working_on: Set up the seed-graph QA census routine: seed_qa_shard.py sharding tool + tests, shards.json manifest, seed_qa_audit.md methodology doc, and rewrite auto_target.json to the T-SEED-QA target (19 shard sessions + closeout).
outcome_summary: Set up the T-SEED-QA seed-graph QA census routine: seed_qa_shard.py (deterministic pre-sharding tool, 26 pytests) + shards.json manifest (516 pedagogical_prerequisite edges + 380 nodes partitioned into 19 shards) + seed_qa_audit.md (class-3 audit master-plan doc, fixed C1/C2/C3 rubric, registered in build_readiness/README.md) + auto_target.json rewritten from the fully-complete T-PHASE-5-AUDIT to T-SEED-QA (19 shard tasks + closeout). Plan-mode pushback surfaced that the Phase 5 production audit already answered C1 at a 13% defect rate; user adjudicated gap-focus (C2 teaching_notes traction + C3 summary cold-readability are net-new) + C1 as an independent drift check, and a full chunked census over a sample. No ADR — reuses the established audit-routine pattern with a different rubric. Validator: 0 hard-fails, 45 checks, baseline soft-warns only, no new categories; pytest engine/tools 1417/1417. Drawers: decision drawer_paideia_decisions_d5f695977ca376fea86403a0, pushback drawer_paideia_general_e1c96d9b2d8431205e40f678. Boot: prior session S-0155 was an interrupted-shutdown on this worktree (work committed locally but unpushed, register_state left in_progress); the user closed it before S-0156 claimed the slot. The T-SEED-QA routine block executes in S-0157+ (forward-pointer, not a deferral of this session's scope).

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes — all four declared deliverables landed end-to-end: seed_qa_shard.py + 26 pytests, shards.json (516 edges + 380 nodes, 19 shards), seed_qa_audit.md (+ build_readiness/README.md registration), and auto_target.json rewritten to T-SEED-QA. No descope, reorder, or deferral of scoped work. The S-0155 interrupted-session recovery at boot was handled by the user before the slot claim and did not change S-0156's scope.
